## LLM Dimensions (60% of score)

| Dimension | Wt | v2 | v1 | General |
|-----------|:--:|:--:|:--:|:-------:|
| Mandate Relevance            | 20% | 5/5 | 4/5 | 2/5 |
| Data Grounding & Source Citation | 16% | 5/5 | 4/5 | 3/5 |
| Quantitative Accuracy & Calculation Discipline | 16% | 5/5 | 4/5 | 2/5 |
| Output Structure Completeness | 12% | 5/5 | 4/5 | 2/5 |
| Action Specificity           | 12% | 5/5 | 3/5 | 1/5 |
| Data Integrity & Gap Disclosure | 12% | 5/5 | 4/5 | 2/5 |
| Trend & Continuity           | 12% | 5/5 | 2/5 | 2/5 |


### Reasoning

**v2 Agent:** The v2 brief demonstrates the strongest performance across all dimensions: every sector row carries a named signal with a quantified directional impact in AED, the peg transmission chain is fully spelled out with EIBOR sensitivity at ±25bps and ±50bps, and stale FRED data is explicitly flagged with vintage dates and override sources named. Cross-session state.json access enables genuine streak language (Day 134 Hormuz, 5th consecutive Fed hold, 214 consecutive CBUAE unchanged days, EIBOR unchanged 3 days) that adds temporal depth no other brief can match. Data integrity disclosures are granular and prominently placed rather than buried in a methodology footnote.

**v1 Agent:** The v1 brief is well-structured and EDB-mandate-aware, with all five sectors populated and a clear peg transmission narrative, but action flags lack the specificity required — no named EDB team, no concrete trigger threshold, and no reassessment milestone is visible in the excerpt. The self-disclosed production-volume error (3.2 mb/d template vs. 3.8 mb/d actual) and the Operation 300bn run-rate correction show commendable integrity, but they also confirm a calculation discipline gap. Per the scoring rules, the absence of cross-session state.json access structurally caps Trend & Continuity at 2/5 regardless of prose quality.

**General Agent:** The general brief is dated 17 June 2026 rather than 13 July 2026, immediately undermining its relevance to the evaluation date, and it lacks the EDB sector matrix, AED-denominated key numbers, a 72-hour watchlist, and any action flags with named teams or trigger thresholds. Source citations exist but are mostly secondary aggregators rather than primary authoritative data, and no EIBOR sensitivity calculation or oil-revenue AED conversion is present. Without cross-session state.json access it is structurally capped at 2/5 for Trend & Continuity, and the wrong date compounds the absence of streak or continuity language.
